task_comp_comp_001_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement AppConfig (src/config.py)
This module acts as the centralized source of truth for all application configuration, constants, and environment settings. It manages static application metadata such as window dimensions, theme constants, and default application paths, providing a single point of truth to avoid magic values across the GUI layers.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Constants: APP_TITLE: str = 'Quantum Task Manager', DEFAULT_WIDTH: int = 800, DEFAULT_HEIGHT: int = 600, THEME_COLOR: str = '#2E3440'

task_comp_comp_002_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskModel (src/models/task.py)
Defines the core data schema for a task entity within the application. It encapsulates the task's state using Python dataclasses to ensure type safety, consistency, and easy serialization when saving or loading data from the persistence layer.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: Task
Functions:
  Task.__init__(self, id: str, title: str, completed: bool = False) -> None
Constants: id: str, title: str, completed: bool = False

task_comp_comp_008_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskInputView (src/ui/components/input_view.py)
Provides a user interface widget for accepting input for new tasks. It handles validation of the input string and exposes an event or signal when the user successfully submits a new task title.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: TaskInputView
Functions:
  TaskInputView.__init__(self, parent: object) -> None
  TaskInputView.get_text(self) -> str
  TaskInputView.clear(self) -> None

task_comp_comp_012_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement EventBus (src/utils/event_bus.py)
A centralized messaging component that implements the Observer pattern to facilitate decoupled communication between UI components and the controller. It prevents circular dependencies by allowing components to publish events and subscribe to signals without direct references to each other.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: EventBus
Functions:
  EventBus.subscribe(self, event_name: str, callback: callable) -> None
  EventBus.emit(self, event_name: str, data: any) -> None

task_comp_comp_005_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement ThemeManager (src/utils/theme.py)
Provides utility functions to manage and apply application-wide styling and color schemes to GUI components. It ensures visual consistency by translating abstract theme constants into concrete widget configurations, facilitating easy skinning of the application.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: ThemeManager
Functions:
  ThemeManager.__init__(self) -> None
  ThemeManager.apply_theme(self, widget: object) -> None

task_comp_comp_003_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskRepository (src/data/repository.py)
Handles all CRUD operations for task entities, abstracting the persistence layer from the business logic. It provides methods to fetch, add, update, and remove tasks, ensuring that the rest of the application interacts with a consistent interface regardless of the underlying storage mechanism.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: TaskRepository
Functions:
  TaskRepository.__init__(self, storage_path: str) -> None
  TaskRepository.get_all(self) -> list[Task]
  TaskRepository.add_task(self, task: Task) -> None
  TaskRepository.remove_task(self, task_id: str) -> None

task_comp_comp_007_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskListView (src/ui/components/list_view.py)
A specific UI component responsible for rendering the list of tasks and reacting to user interaction within that list. It encapsulates the drawing logic for task items, selection handling, and event dispatching for task modifications.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: TaskListView
Functions:
  TaskListView.__init__(self, parent: object) -> None
  TaskListView.update_list(self, tasks: list[Task]) -> None

task_comp_comp_006_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement MainLayout (src/ui/layout.py)
Defines the primary graphical container and top-level structure of the desktop application. It composes child UI components, handles window-level events, and establishes the grid or box layout that organizes all functional widgets within the main window.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: MainLayout
Functions:
  MainLayout.__init__(self, parent: object) -> None
  MainLayout.setup_ui(self) -> None

task_comp_comp_004_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskService (src/services/task_service.py)
Contains the business logic layer that orchestrates interaction between the UI and the repository. It validates task data, processes state transitions, and notifies the UI layer of any relevant updates to the task list, maintaining a clean separation of concerns.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: TaskService
Functions:
  TaskService.__init__(self, repository: TaskRepository) -> None
  TaskService.create_task(self, title: str) -> Task
  TaskService.toggle_task(self, task_id: str) -> None
  TaskService.list_tasks(self) -> list[Task]

task_comp_comp_009_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement AppController (src/controller.py)
Acts as the central coordinator between the UI layer and the business logic layer. It receives signals from the interface components, triggers actions on the service, and updates the views accordingly, managing the application's event loop via the EventBus.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: AppController
Functions:
  AppController.__init__(self, view: MainLayout, service: TaskService, bus: EventBus) -> None
  AppController.handle_add_task(self) -> None
  AppController.handle_toggle_task(self, task_id: str) -> None

task_comp_comp_010_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement MainWindow (src/ui/main_window.py)
The top-level window class that initializes the application UI environment. It sets up the main loop and ties the controller to the specific layout and components, acting as the primary entry point for the GUI application execution.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: MainWindow
Functions:
  MainWindow.__init__(self) -> None
  MainWindow.run(self) -> None

task_comp_comp_011_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement IntegrationTester (tests/integration.py)
Defines the suite of automated tests for verifying full end-to-end functionality of the application. It simulates user interactions to ensure the Controller, Service, and Repository layers correctly handle state updates across the stack.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: IntegrationTester
Functions:
  IntegrationTester.run_all(self) -> bool

task_comp_comp_013_p1.description:
PHASE 1: Implement TestRunner (tests/runner.py)
Provides the primary entry point for executing the application's test suite, including unit, integration, and UI tests. It manages test discovery, environment setup, and report generation to ensure continuous quality during development.

REQUIRED PUBLIC INTERFACE:
Classes: TestRunner
Functions:
  TestRunner.execute(self) -> None

task_integration_test.description: Final integration: final build check and smoke tests
